claim: Vision transformers (ViT) beat CNNs not because of architecture but because of massive ViT-specific pretraining like MAE and DINOv2
actionable: When choosing vision models, prioritize models with strong self-supervised pretraining (MAE, DINOv2) over architectural choices

claim: Masked autoencoder (MAE) pretraining allows models to learn spatial inductive biases by reconstructing dropped patches, but is ViT-specific and cannot apply to CNNs
actionable: Use MAE-pretrained ViT models for vision tasks where you need strong unsupervised spatial understanding

claim: Flash Attention and other LLM speedups from the transformer era eliminated the performance advantage of Hiera over ViT by removing the main speedup Hiera offered
actionable: When evaluating vision transformer performance, ensure you test with flash attention or modern attention kernels as they narrow performance gaps

claim: Foundation models like SAM are expensive to deploy (800M parameters, 300ms on T4) because they trade deployment flexibility for pretraining power
actionable: For edge deployment or latency-constrained scenarios, use neural architecture search (like RF-DETR) to generate smaller variants from large foundation models rather than using them directly

claim: Roboflow achieved 40x speedup for object detection vs fine-tuning SAM 3 directly by adapting foundation models with neural architecture search
actionable: Use NAS techniques to adapt large pretrained vision models to your specific hardware and latency constraints

claim: DINOv3 self-supervised features are as good as fully supervised learning features when probed with just a linear layer
actionable: Use DINOv3-pretrained ViT features for transfer learning; they're strong enough to compete with supervised baselines with minimal fine-tuning
